Iran Moves to Tap Key Asian Markets as U.S. Waives Oil Sanctions
headlines.summary: Iran is pitching its oil to Asian buyers outside China, contacting India, South Korea, and Japan, as the United States issued a temporary two-month waiver allowing Iranian oil sales, including in U.S. dollars, until August 21. headlines.title: Oil Prices Continue to Slide as Peace Talks Progress
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices extended their decline today, with Brent crude sliding to $77.51 per barrel at the time of writing, and West Texas Intermediate at $73.62 per barrel, on reports that the United States and Iran were making progress in their peace talks.
